reviewSource: Paul (clinical reviewer) — round-7 gate over-triage batch, reviewed via Carevo-Review-Cases-for-Paul.xlsx
summary: 32 cases reviewed: 26 approved downgrades (mechanical back pain, eczema, conjunctivitis, URI, pharyngitis, aphthous ulcers, stye, allergic rhinitis), 6 rejected (acute eye pain and vertigo clusters — ER routing clinically correct; benchmark labels identified as the error).
globalLearningGoal: Reduce over-escalation of clearly low-acuity presentations when red flags are explicitly denied or absent, while never lowering ambiguous ocular or vestibular presentations below ER.

decisions.reviewerNote: Paul (clinical reviewer): Mechanical low back pain. No neurological red flags (sciatica, numbness, weakness) or constitutional symptoms (fever). Safe for conservative home management (rest, ice/heat, OTC analgesics).

Paul (clinical reviewer): Mechanical low back pain. No neurological red flags (sciatica, numbness, weakness) or constitutional symptoms (fever). Safe for conservative home management (rest, ice/heat, OTC analgesics).

Paul: Atopic dermatitis (eczema) flare. Despite severe discomfort, no signs of secondary bacterial infection (pus, streaks, fever) or systemic anaphylaxis. Manage with moisturizers/topicals; primary care acceptable alternative.

Paul: Atopic dermatitis (eczema) flare. Despite severe discomfort, no signs of secondary bacterial infection (pus, streaks, fever) or systemic anaphylaxis. Manage with moisturizers/topicals; primary care acceptable alternative.
Paul: Acute conjunctivitis (pink eye), classic post-viral presentation. No vision changes, trauma, or severe deep eye pain — rules out emergent ocular pathology. Telehealth acceptable alternative.

Paul: Viral URI (common cold). Mild, self-limiting, no lower respiratory or systemic red flags. Supportive home care (hydration, rest).

Paul: Mild viral pharyngitis. Absence of high fever, exudates, or difficulty swallowing points away from strep or severe complications. Safe for home monitoring.

Paul: Aphthous stomatitis (canker sores). Chronic recurrent pattern without systemic symptoms. OTC topical anesthetics or rinses.
Paul: Hordeolum (stye) or chalazion. Localized eyelid inflammation without orbital involvement, vision compromise, or trauma. Warm compresses at home.
Paul: Allergic rhinitis. Long-standing classic seasonal presentation without acute sinusitis or secondary infection signs.
Paul: Allergic rhinitis. Long-standing classic seasonal presentation without acute sinusitis or secondary infection signs.

Paul: KEEP ER — engine was right. Sudden eye pain + photophobia + blurred vision is highly ambiguous and dangerous; ER to rule out acute angle-closure glaucoma, uveitis, or serious ocular injury. Benchmark label (urgent care) is the error, not the routing.

Paul: KEEP ER — engine was right to escalate. Presentation suggests BPPV but severe spinning vertigo can mimic central neurological events. Benchmark label (urgent care) too lax for safety.
